title: Read Only Safety and Query Limits
summary: Use read-only mode and tool allow-lists. Prefer KQL query statements over management or control commands. Allow harmless .
analogy: A read-only rifle range still has range rules: aim downrange, limit rounds, identify target, and stop when ordered.
diagram:
flowchart TD
  Policy[Safety policy] --> Identity[Least privilege MI]
  Policy --> Tools[Allowed Kusto tools]
  Policy --> KQL[Read only KQL]
  Policy --> Limits[Rows and timeout]
  Policy --> Audit[Tool call logs]
body:
## First principles

An analytics agent can still cause harm without writing data: expensive scans, sensitive data exposure, or accidental control commands can create operational and compliance incidents.

## Definition

Use read-only mode and tool allow-lists. Prefer KQL query statements over management or control commands. Allow harmless .show metadata commands for discovery, but block destructive or permission-changing commands unless a human-admin workflow explicitly owns them. Cap result size with take or top, use time filters, and configure query timeouts.

## How it fits together

Safety stack: least-privilege identity, narrow MCP tools, approval for risky calls, KQL linting for time windows and row caps, server-side timeout, and audit logging.

## Worked example

```kql
AppTraces
| where Timestamp between (ago(24h) .. now())
| where SeverityLevel >= 3
| project Timestamp, OperationName, Message, SeverityLevel
| take 100
```

*Safer query pattern with time window and row cap.*

drills.a: To cap returned rows and reduce accidental data exposure or oversized responses.
No. .show metadata commands are generally read-oriented, but many management commands can change state or permissions.

drills.q: What should happen before a high-risk tool call?

teachItBack: Read-only is more than not writing rows. I also limit scope, time, rows, and tools. The safest Kusto agent uses least privilege, approval, timeouts, and logs.

glossary.definition: A Kusto command that manages metadata, security, ingestion, or other service state.
Maximum duration allowed for a query before cancellation.
A limit on how many rows a query returns.
